response.justification: The candidate's profile is fundamentally misaligned with this C++ developer role. Despite a career spanning roughly 15 years, C++ appears only in a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; it is conspicuously absent from the current skills list. The candidate's active expertise centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — web-oriented stacks with no meaningful overlap with the embedded/industrial C++ demanded here. There is no evidence of experience with IEC 62443, SDL practices, MQTT, industrial protocols, multi-threaded systems design, or industrial automation environments.
strengths: Brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on LabWindows/CVI provides at least a passing familiarity with engineering-grade tooling
High-performance, low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby (async I/O, fibers, millions of HTTP req/s) demonstrates awareness of performance-critical design
Event-driven, open-source GIS platform (GISCollective) and distributed-systems background shows some architectural breadth relevant to IoT-adjacent thinking
concerns: C++ has not been used professionally for over a decade and is not listed among current skills — a disqualifying gap for the primary technical requirement
No experience with IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, MQTT, or any industrial protocol (OPC UA, Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
Entire recent career (2018–present) is JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js/React — completely orthogonal to the role's core stack and domain
